title: 10 Herbal Teas Proven to Help with Tinnitus
intro_desc: Herbal teas for tinnitus are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the symptoms of tinnitus, a condition characterized by ringing, buzzing, or other sounds in the ears. These teas are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that promote ear health, reduce inflammation, and improve blood circulation, ultimately relieving tinnitus symptoms. The best herbal teas for tinnitus include Ginkgo biloba, Melissa officinalis, Zingiber officinale, Valeriana officinalis, Avena sativa, Passiflora incarnata, Lavandula angustifolia, Hypericum perforatum, Bacopa monnieri, and Centella asiatica. If left untreated, tinnitus can significantly impact daily life, causing anxiety, depression, sleep disturbances, and decreased quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is a popular choice for people with tinnitus because of its potential to improve blood flow to the ears. Tinnitus is often caused by poor circulation and damage to the tiny blood vessels in the ears. Ginkgo biloba contains flavonoids and terpenoids that can help widen these blood vessels and improve blood flow. This increased blood flow can bring more oxygen and nutrients to the ears, which may help reduce the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has anti-inflammatory properties that may also help reduce inflammation in the ears, which can contribute to tinnitus. Some studies suggest that Ginkgo biloba may even help reduce the severity of tinnitus symptoms, making it a potentially useful natural remedy for people who suffer from this condition. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba tea may also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the ears from damage and further reduce the risk of tinnitus.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the inflammation in the inner ear, which is often a contributing factor to tinnitus.
Bilobalide is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce damage to the inner ear and promote healing, which can help to alleviate symptoms of tinnitus.

Isorhapontigenin is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce damage to the inner ear's hair cells and alleviate the ringing sensation associated with tinnitus.
Kaempferol is good for tinnitus because it has antioxidant properties that can protect the tiny hair cells in the ear from damage, helping to reduce the ringing sensation associated with tinnitus.

remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for tinnitus
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea that can help with tinnitus, start by gathering dried Yarrow flowers or purchasing them from a health food store. Use one teaspoon of dried Yarrow flowers for every cup of boiling water you're using. Place the Yarrow flowers in a heat-resistant cup or teapot, and pour boiling water over them. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, so the active ingredients can release into the water. After steeping, strain the liquid to remove the Yarrow flowers, and discard the solids. You can drink the tea as is, or add a little honey if you prefer a sweeter taste. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of Yarrow tea per day to potentially alleviate tinnitus symptoms. However, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies, especially if you're taking any medications or have underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a popular herbal remedy that may help with tinnitus. This is because the tea contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Inflammation and oxidative stress in the ear can contribute to tinnitus symptoms, such as ringing or buzzing in the ears. Drinking Melissa officinalis tea may help to reduce this inflammation and stress, potentially providing relief from tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, the tea's calming effects may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can sometimes exacerbate tinnitus. Some people find that drinking a cup of Melissa officinalis tea before bed helps them to relax and sleep better, which can also be beneficial for tinnitus sufferers. While more research is needed to fully understand the effects of Melissa officinalis tea on tinnitus, it may be worth trying for those looking for a natural remedy.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid has been found to be good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the inflammation and damage in the inner ear, which is often associated with tinnitus.
Luteolin is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce damage to the inner ear and promote healing, leading to relief from tinnitus symptoms.

Apigenin is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which can alleviate tinnitus symptoms such as ringing and buzzing.

remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for tinnitus
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea that may help with tinnitus, start by gathering the dried leaves and flowers of the plant, also known as yarrow. You can find dried yarrow at a health food store or online. Begin by boiling a cup of water and then remove it from the heat. Add a teaspoon of dried yarrow to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the tea will be. After the steeping time has passed, strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, and discard the solids. Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking it. It is recommended to drink the tea 2-3 times a day to see the benefits. You can also add honey to the tea if you prefer it sweeter. Note that while yarrow tea may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment, especially if you are already taking medications or have underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, may help with tinnitus due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Tinnitus is a condition where people hear sounds that aren't there, often caused by damage to the inner ear or hearing nerves. Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the ear, which could alleviate tinnitus symptoms. The antioxidants in ginger tea may also help protect the ear from further damage and promote healing. Ginger tea has been traditionally used to relieve pain and reduce nausea, which can also be beneficial for people with tinnitus. Additionally, ginger's natural properties may help improve blood flow to the ear, which could help restore balance to the inner ear and reduce tinnitus symptoms. Some people have reported that drinking ginger tea regularly has helped them manage their tinnitus, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols may be good for tinnitus because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the ears and help to alleviate the ringing or buzzing associated with tinnitus.
Shogaols are good for tinnitus because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce ringing in the ears by protecting the hair cells in the inner ear from damage.
Polyphenols are good for tinnitus because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Curcuminoids are good for tinnitus because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the ear, which may help to alleviate tinnitus symptoms by blocking the abnormal nerve signals that cause ringing or buzzing sounds.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for tinnitus
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal teas that help with tinnitus, start by gathering dried or fresh Achillea millefolium flowers. If using fresh flowers, dry them first by placing them in a low-temperature oven (150°F - 200°F) for a few hours or by air-drying them. If using dried flowers, skip this step. Measure one teaspoon of the dried flowers for every cup of boiling water. Place the flowers in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup and pour in the boiling water. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and discard the flowers. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's recommended to drink the tea without any additives for better results. Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, ideally before meals, to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tea, also known as valerian root tea, may help with tinnitus. Tinnitus is a condition where people hear sounds, like ringing or buzzing, when there is no actual noise. This can be caused by stress, anxiety, or problems with the ears. Valerian root has been used for centuries to calm the mind and body. It contains chemicals that help to relax the muscles and slow down brain activity, which can reduce stress and anxiety. By reducing stress and anxiety, valerian root tea may help to decrease the symptoms of tinnitus. Some people also use valerian root tea to improve sleep quality, as tinnitus can be worse when a person is tired or stressed. Drinking valerian root tea before bed may help to promote a good night's sleep and reduce tinnitus symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid has been shown to be effective in reducing tinnitus by blocking the overactive nerve signals in the brain that cause ringing or buzzing sounds, and by promoting relaxation and reducing stress that can exacerbate the condition.
Valeranone is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the ear, which may contribute to tinnitus symptoms.
Valerenal is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.

Valerenol has been found to be good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for tinnitus, and also has a sedative effect that can help to calm the auditory system and alleviate the ringing sounds associated with the condition.

Isovaleric acid is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the inflammation in the auditory nerve and inner ear, which is often a cause of tinnitus symptoms.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea for tinnitus relief, you'll need dried yarrow flowers, boiling water, and a teapot. Start by measuring one teaspoon of dried yarrow flowers for every cup of water you plan to use, and add them to your teapot. Pour boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the liquid. Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the flowers, and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's best to consume the tea without any additives for maximum benefits. Once cooled, you can drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms, as Achillea millefolium is known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe the ear and reduce ringing sounds.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as lavender tea, has been studied for its potential to help with tinnitus. Tinnitus is a condition where people hear ringing or other sounds in their ears when there's no outside noise. This can be very annoying and affect daily life. Some research suggests that lavender tea may help with tinnitus because it has calming effects on the body and mind. Lavender tea contains chemicals that can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for tinnitus. When we're stressed or anxious, our ears can pick up on internal sounds more easily, making tinnitus worse. Drinking lavender tea may help calm the nervous system, reducing the sensitivity of the ears and making it easier to ignore internal sounds. Additionally, lavender tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the ears that can contribute to tinnitus. Some people have reported that drinking lavender tea helps them relax and sleep better, which can also help with tinnitus symptoms.

Linalyl acetate is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can alleviate the ringing sensations associated with tinnitus.

Rosmarinic acid is good for tinnitus because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce damage to the hair cells in the ear and promote healing, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Apigenin is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of tinnitus, allowing for a potential reduction in the symptoms.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea that helps with tinnitus, start by gathering fresh or dried yarrow flowers, which are the flowers of the Achillea millefolium plant. You can usually find yarrow in health food stores or grow it in your garden. If using fresh flowers, gently rinse them in cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, combine 1 tablespoon of yarrow flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has cooled slightly. Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the flowers. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but avoid adding milk, as it may reduce the tea's effectiveness. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms, as the anti-inflammatory properties in yarrow may help reduce inflammation in the ears and promote relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tea is a natural remedy that may help people with tinnitus. This type of tea is made from the passionflower plant, which has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. Tinnitus is often linked to stress and anxiety, so using Passiflora incarnata tea might help to calm the mind and body, reducing the symptoms of tinnitus. The tea contains chemicals that can help slow down the activity in the brain, which might make tinnitus sound less loud or disturbing. Additionally, some studies suggest that Passiflora incarnata tea may help improve sleep quality, which is often a problem for people with tinnitus. By improving sleep and reducing stress, Passiflora incarnata tea might help people with tinnitus feel more relaxed and focused, making it easier to manage their condition.

If using fresh, gently rinse them in cold water, then pat dry with a clean cloth. If using dried, simply measure out a teaspoon for every cup of boiling water you plan to use. Combine the yarrow with a cup of boiling water in a teapot or mug, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. You can then strain the tea into another cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, to remove the plant material. Let the tea cool slightly, then drink it as needed, ideally 2-3 times a day. Some people find it helpful to drink yarrow tea before bed, as it may promote a restful night's sleep and reduce tinnitus symptoms in the morning.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tea, also known as oat straw tea, is a natural remedy that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms. Tinnitus is a condition where people hear ringing, buzzing, or other sounds in their ears when there is no external source. Avena sativa is a type of oat that has been used for centuries in herbal medicine to calm the nervous system and reduce stress. When consumed as a tea, it is believed to soothe and calm the nerves in the ear, which may help to reduce the perception of tinnitus sounds. Additionally, Avena sativa has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation in the ear that can contribute to tinnitus. Some people also use it to improve sleep quality, as sleep deprivation can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Avena sativa tea may be a helpful natural remedy for people experiencing tinnitus.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides are good for tinnitus because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which in turn can help to alleviate the symptoms of tinnitus.

Polyphenols have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, making them beneficial for treating tinnitus.

It's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using this tea as a treatment for tinnitus, especially if you're already taking any medications. remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, is a type of herb that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues. The tea made from this herb is believed to be beneficial for people who suffer from tinnitus, or ringing in the ears. This is because St. John's Wort contains chemicals that can help to reduce inflammation and fight off free radicals in the body, which can contribute to tinnitus. The tea may also help to improve blood flow to the ears and reduce stress, which can also contribute to tinnitus. Additionally, the antioxidants found in St. John's Wort can help to protect the delicate hair cells in the ears from damage, which can also contribute to tinnitus. Some people find that drinking St. John's Wort tea regularly can help to reduce the severity and frequency of tinnitus symptoms. However, more research is needed to fully understand the effects of this herb on tinnitus.

Rosemary's antioxidant properties may also protect the ears from damage caused by free radicals. Some people find that drinking rosemary tea helps to calm their nerves and reduce the intensity of tinnitus symptoms. If using fresh, choose young leaves and flowers and wash them thoroughly with clean water. If using dried, store-bought or homemade, make sure to check for any signs of mold or contamination. Combine 1-2 teaspoons of the Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Let it steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup. Discard the solids and your tea is ready to drink. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming it, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking any medications. Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day to potentially alleviate tinnitus symptoms, but it's essential to note that individual results may vary.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow tea, is a herbal remedy that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms. Tinnitus is a condition where people hear ringing, buzzing, or other noises in their ears even when there's no sound around. Yarrow tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the ear's blood vessels. This swelling can cause the nerves to become irritated, leading to tinnitus. The antioxidants in yarrow tea may also help protect the ear's delicate nerve endings from damage. Additionally, yarrow tea has been traditionally used to treat ear problems, such as infections and congestion, which can contribute to tinnitus. Some research suggests that yarrow's flavonoids may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the ear. Drinking yarrow tea may help soothe and calm the ear, potentially reducing the severity of tinnitus symptoms. The tea is also easy to make and has a mild, pleasant taste.

Apigenin is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce damage to the inner ear and promote healthy hearing.
Luteolin is good for tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce damage to the delicate hair cells in the ear and help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal teas for tinnitus
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea that helps with tinnitus, start by gathering dried Achillea millefolium flowers, also known as yarrow. Begin by boiling a cup of water in a pot, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers to the boiling water. Allow the flowers to steep in the hot water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth. You can also add honey to taste if you prefer your tea sweet. After straining the tea, let it cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of yarrow tea per day to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms. Some people find that drinking yarrow tea before bedtime helps improve their sleep quality and reduces tinnitus symptoms. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using yarrow tea as a treatment for tinnitus, especially if you're already taking medications or have underlying health conditions.

supplementary_best: A combination of Ginkgo biloba, Melissa officinalis, and Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas may help relieve tinnitus symptoms. Ginkgo biloba is known to improve blood flow to the ears and promote healing, reducing tinnitus severity. Melissa officinalis, or lemon balm, has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease ear inflammation, which may contribute to tinnitus. Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender, has a calming effect on the nervous system and can reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for tinnitus. This combination addresses the physical and emotional aspects of tinnitus, making it a well-rounded remedy.
supplementary_avoid: If you have tinnitus, it's a good idea to avoid herbal teas that can make it worse. One such herb is 'Ephedra sinica', also known as Ma Huang. It contains a chemical called ephedrine, which can increase blood pressure and worsen tinnitus symptoms. Another herb to avoid is 'Yohimbe bark', which also contains a chemical that can raise blood pressure and make tinnitus worse.  'Echinacea' tea can also be a problem for people with tinnitus. It can stimulate the nervous system and make tinnitus symptoms worse. Similarly, 'Saw palmetto' tea may not be suitable for people with tinnitus. It can affect the way the body processes certain chemicals that may worsen tinnitus symptoms. 'Ginseng' tea, particularly 'Panax ginseng', should be consumed with caution as it can increase blood pressure and worsen tinnitus.  'Yarrow' and 'Sassafras' teas may also be problematic for people with tinnitus. They can cause or worsen the condition due to their ability to stimulate the nervous system. However, it's essential to note that individual reactions to these teas can vary, and some people may experience no issues at all.
supplementary_other_remedies: Some people find relief from tinnitus by using essential oils. Peppermint oil, for example, can help calm the mind and reduce stress, which may contribute to the ringing sound. When inhaled, the oil's active compounds can affect the brain's perception of sound, making it easier to ignore the tinnitus. Similarly, some people use sound therapy, which is a form of auditory training that helps the brain adjust to the tinnitus and learn to filter it out. This can be done through white noise machines or apps that generate soothing sounds. Another natural remedy that may help is dietary changes, particularly a low-sodium diet. Some research suggests that high salt intake can make tinnitus worse, possibly because it can cause blood vessels to swell, leading to increased pressure on the inner ear.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with tinnitus often experience other health issues. Some common ones include hearing loss, anxiety, depression, insomnia, and high blood pressure.  Hearing loss can be caused by age, loud noises, or ear infections. Medicinal herbs like Ginkgo biloba and Ashwagandha may help. Ginkgo biloba improves blood flow to the ears, while Ashwagandha reduces stress and inflammation.  Anxiety and depression can be related to tinnitus. The constant ringing or buzzing in the ears can cause emotional distress. Herbs like Passionflower and St. John's Wort may help. Passionflower calms the nervous system, while St. John's Wort lifts mood.  Insomnia is common in people with tinnitus. The ringing in the ears can make it hard to fall asleep. Herbs like Valerian root and Lavender may help. Valerian root promotes relaxation, while Lavender calms the mind.  High blood pressure often accompanies tinnitus. This can be due to stress and inflammation. Herbs like Garlic and Hawthorn may help. Garlic lowers blood pressure, while Hawthorn strengthens heart health.  Inflammation is a common factor in many tinnitus-related health issues. Herbs like Turmeric and Ginger may help. Turmeric reduces inflammation, while Ginger soothes the digestive system.  These medicinal herbs work by reducing inflammation, calming the nervous system, promoting relaxation, and improving blood flow. They may not cure tinnitus, but they can help manage related health issues.
